LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT CRANE CO.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. ARGUED AND THE COURT RULED AS FOLLOWS: THE COURT DID NOT CONSIDER THE REPLY. HOWEVER, IF IT DID, THE COURT WOULD NOT CONCLUDE THAT THERE OUGHT TO BE A FURTHER OPPORTUNITY TO SUBMIT ADDITIONAL EVIDENCE RELATED TO THE TESTIMONY OF MR. WALTERS. CRANE CO. SHIFTED THE BURDEN OF PROOF BASED ON THE SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES THAT IT PROPOUNDED. WALTERS DECLARATION IS NOT SIGNED, THEREFORE NOT ADMISSIBLE. HOWEVER, IF IT WERE ADMISSIBLE, THAT DECLARATION DOES NOT CHANGE THE STATE OF THE RECORD ON WHICH CRANE CO. MOVED. FOR THE REASONS STATED, THE MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS GRANTED. THE COURT RULED ON CRANE CO.'S OBJECTIONS TO PLAINTIFF'S EVIDENCE, ORDER SIGNED. (302/PJM) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR #8600

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Oct-12-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT CRANE CO.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION.
APPEARANCES: Mark A. Swanson, Esq. & Anthony D. Prince, Esq. of Paul, Hanley & Harley LLP 510/559-9980 for pltfs & opposing parties; Geoff M. Davis, Esq. of Kirkpatrick & Lockhart Preston Gates Ellis LLP 249-1003 for deft & moving party Crane Co.
RULING: Argued and the Court ruled as follows: The Court did not consider the reply. However, if it did, the Court would not conclude that there ought to be a further opportunity to submit additional evidence related to the testimony of Mr. Walters. Crane Co. shifted the burden of proof based on the special interrogatories that it propounded. Walters declaration is not signed, therefore not admissible. However, if it were admissible, that declaration does not change the state of the record on which Crane Co. moved. For the reasons stated, the motion for summary judgment is granted. The Court ruled on Crane Co.'s objections to plaintiff's evidence, order signed. (302/PJM/VC) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR #8600
